what: M=64 fused path: BLOCK_SIZE_K=384 with num_stages=2, keeping 16x128 tile and waves_per_eu=2
why: The fresh BM still shows 64x7168x2048 as the largest fused-path contributor at 13.1us. BSK=256 uses 8 K-iterations; BSK=512 was historically too coarse, and BSK=128 failed structurally. BSK=384 is the only untested midpoint that may trim loop/control overhead while preserving double-buffered overlap.
override: Prior M64 block-size attempts covered BSK=256 baseline, BSK=512 regressions, and an invalid BSK=128 path. BSK=384 on the current v211 baseline remains unmeasured.

what: M=64 fused path: keep 16x128x256 tile but reduce num_warps from 4 to 2
why: v224 showed non-power-of-two BLOCK_SIZE_K values are invalid in the preshuffle kernel, so the remaining isolated M=64 knob is warp count. The fresh baseline still has M=64 at 13.1us; reducing num_warps may lower register pressure while preserving the same 224-block grid and double-buffered K pipeline.
override: Previous NW=2 results were on different M64 tile families or bundled with other tile changes. This isolates num_warps=2 on the current v211 16x128x256 path.

what: waves_per_eu=1 for M<=32 K<=1024 fused path (from 2) on the v211 cache=None baseline
why: The two M=32 K=512 benchmark shapes are still material contributors at 6.40us and 6.21us. v211 proved this path is sensitive to cache behavior; the remaining unmeasured occupancy point is waves_per_eu=1, which may relax register pressure slightly without fully giving up the explicit occupancy hint.
override: A prior session reserved and test-passed this exact idea as v221 but never benchmarked or submitted it due a coordinator reset. This run measures it properly on the current baseline.

what: Split-K path: use BLOCK_SIZE_K=128 with NUM_KSPLIT=8 for the 16x2112x7168 shape
why: In aiter's get_splitk() normalization, NUM_KSPLIT=8 with BSK=256 collapses back to the current 7-way config, which is why prior split-8 tests were aliases. Setting BSK=128 yields a genuinely different config: SPLITK_BLOCK_SIZE=896, ACTUAL_KSPLIT=8, 272 blocks instead of 238, and smaller K chunks that may improve occupancy on the 16x2112x7168 path.
override: Prior split-8 attempts used BSK=256 and normalized to the current 7-way config. This is the first real 8-way split-K test enabled by BSK=128.

what: Split-K path: use BLOCK_SIZE_K=512 with NUM_KSPLIT=2 for the 16x2112x7168 shape
why: This is the opposite end of the split-K tradeoff from the current 7-way setup: far fewer partials and less reduction work, but much longer per-split K loops. The history covers split-4, split-7, split-8 alias, and split-14, but not a valid 2-way / BSK512 configuration.
override: Prior BSK512 work focused on split-14 library config or other shapes. This isolates the untried 2-way / BSK512 configuration for 16x2112x7168.

what: Split-K reduce: pass MAX_KSPLIT=ACTUAL_KSPLIT (7) instead of next_power_of_2(NUM_KSPLIT)=8
why: The current split-K path computes 7 real partials but pads the gluon reduce kernel to 8 lanes, loading one masked extra slice. If the reduce kernel accepts non-power-of-two MAX_KSPLIT, this removes useless work without touching the main GEMM configuration.
override: No prior attempt targeted the MAX_KSPLIT padding behavior specifically; past reduce tuning changed tile sizes or kernel families, not the reduction extent itself.
